title: Evangelion Unit 01 is an it
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Unit 01 is an "it"             Quote Post by Solid627 » Mon Aug 18, 2008 6:48 am To clarify some things. Evangelion Unit 01 is an "it", neither male nor female. Although, base on the bodily proportions and, notwithstanding, the consideration of wide shoulders opposing narrow hips, it strongly implies muscular and athletic male characteristics. It is an " it ". The word Eva does not stand for Eve in the context of "Adam and Eve". The word Eva is short for Evangelion/EvANGELICal. It is implicitly biblical including The Holy Message /Message of Salvation. In the manga translation, or possible mistranslation, it was inferred to as a "she" but not in a literal sense. A captain or a pirate may affectionately refer to his battleship as a woman, e.g. "...She is sinking..." But a weapon is a weapon, and it is sexless. It is an "it". Angels are sexless beings and are categorized based solely on their functions. Some angels are messengers. Some angels are Demons, generally understood as fallen angels, formerly of God. Another type of angels are Archangels (Lat. archangelus, pl. archangeli) are superior or higher-ranking angels, they exist to fight wars. Apparently, this Eva Unit 01 including all the other Eva units, is leaning more towards either being an Archangel or a Demon...or a Combination of both...an Archangel with a Demonic presence, an "Oni". But more importantly, in this anime these "Units" are purely biological weapons, a calculated mix of organic and technological advancement utilized strategically for whenever the situation demands it. Again, it is an " it ". Neither " he " nor " she ".[/b]

[]: Re: Evangelion Unit 01 is an "it"             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 18, 2008 7:14 am Solid627 wrote: To clarify some things.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Mon Aug 18, 2008 8:19 am It depends on the language. In polish ships are masculine. ;] But English lacks grammatical gender so you won't understand.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Aug 18, 2008 9:12 am it's female because Yui is in it, for starters

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Aug 18, 2008 9:34 am How come it is female, aside from the soul coming from a female? I thought genders of living animals are determined by what reproductive organs they have?

[]:             Quote Post by Solid627 » Mon Aug 18, 2008 10:17 am ...honto ni? Just to clear things up. Does it ever occur to us why the girl Ayanami Rei looks like Ayanami Rei? Who made her look like that so? It was Ikari Gendou, who is obviously obssessed with being reunited together again with his, supposedly, deceased wife, Ikari Yui, at all costs, using Machiavellian tactics, even planning on sacrificing his only begotten son, Ikari Shinji. Take note: Ikari Gendou... created Ayanami Rei. Ayanami Rei is made by Ikari Gendou to look identical to his wife Ikari Yui. During an experiment Ikari Yui got absorbed either intentionally or accidentally by Unit-01 which is an "it". Unit-01 is simply a biological weapon derived from Lilith which is an alien tool/weapon designed for galactic conquest. Lilith is one of the sexless biological weapons "merely branded as Lilith by a secret sort of MK-Ultra organization with biblical references using biblical/Kabbalah (I was a member of this sect) terms to make the alien information more digestible to this human organization, thereby getting a sense of control and helping this organization to build feasible plans to counter the alien's corrective measures to retrieve Lilith, the other type of weapon was merely "branded" Adam, another sexless biological weapon, and both weapons were not suppose to be dispatched in the same planet.. However, there was an accident with the trajectory of the container of the weapon, merely branded by humans as Lilith, the moon-shaped container accidentally hit the Earth right at the very heart of Japan. (Not in America but in Japan, the land of mecha.) Later this container became the Geofront. The angel attack were merely the alien's corrective measures to retrieve Lilith, a sexless weapon that they did not intend to crash in the same planet as the other weapon "branded by humans as Adam". Both are different types of sexless weapons dispatched throughout the universe designed by the supposedly First Ancestral Race(merely branded by a secret human organization that has nothing to do with the aliens in the first place, nor are the aliens aware of it), with the sole purpose of subduing portions of the universe and to dominate and establish total control of the galaxy in a calculated manner. Rei is just the by-product intentionally made by Ikari Gendou using a mixture of angel/alien material and some salvaged parts of Ikari Yui, and Gendou-san made it so with a specific plan in mind, to temporarily relieve the grief due to the unbearable absence of his beloved wife Ikari Yui and to control the outcome of Human Instrumentality and possibly redirect the chains of events and be re-united with the only person that he really trully cares about... the only person that connected to him, the only manifestation that he is capable of love, again Ikari Yui. He is that single-mindedly obsessed with his wife. Ayanami Rei looks like that simply to remind him of Ikari Yui and of his sole objective, that is to bring back his wife. Fact is, Ikari Gendo was born as Gendo Rokubungi. Because of his extreme love for his wife he adopted her name Ikari, that is why he became Ikari Gendou... which is an extreme and rare act especially in a male dominated Japanese culture. To Gendou-san the end justifies the means. Apparently, the aliens branded as Angels existed prior to the human female Ikari Yui. And Angels are merely given familiar names in biblical connotations, using the text retrieved from the Dead Sea Scroll that's possibly made by a secret human organization planning to control/hijack this alien technology, redirect the flow of information, and to sublimate such power to further their self-interests. Well...If you were to... become an alien and invade another country... and the local people, who doesn't share your language and cannot communicate with you at all, who doesn't know your background or where you came from... started to "Brand" you using their own language, using their own terms based on their own established belief systems. Would you even have a clue why they called you "this" and "that"? It has nothing to do with you or your purpose of why you are there in the first place... you are there to carry out your objective. Does that make it clear...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 18, 2008 11:01 am Actually, the Evas ARE female because the souls within them are female. Also keep in mind the Eva don't automatically have souls as Ritsuko stated in episode 23' when she also mentioned the Evas are human. I don't see how kanojo (she) is a mistranslation when the manga and anime both state this. They actually SAY the Japanese word for "she" in both versions of the scene! Also I'm paraphrasing with those but you're forgetting about two different lines in the show that state otherwise about Lilith and Adam. In episode 25 of the TV series Gendo calls Lilith as "mother" in the line about how Instrumentality is "just the return to our mother who was lost from this world". In episode 24' when Kaworu floats to Lilith and is talking about Adam, he states "Adam, our mother . Must all those who were born of Adam return? etc". Apparently, the aliens branded as Angels existed

[]: Unit-01 is an "it"             Quote Post by Solid627 » Mon Aug 18, 2008 11:09 am V wrote: it's female because Yui is in it, for starters

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 18, 2008 11:19 am Evangelion Unit 01 is an "it", neither male nor female. Although, base on the bodily proportions and, notwithstanding, the consideration of wide shoulders opposing narrow hips, it strongly implies muscular and athletic male characteristics. It is an "it".

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Mon Aug 18, 2008 11:22 am Please drop the weeaboo shit. SUPAAKAWAIINEKODESU ~~~~~<3

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 18, 2008 11:35 am AuraTwilight wrote: LOL wrong Angels, buddy.

[]: Re: Unit-01 is an "it"             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 18, 2008 11:38 am Solid627 wrote: Soo desu... Ikari Yui's soul was absorbed by the alien organism. And somehow can manage to awaken the latent alien being from time to time. Especially, when it concerns her son's well being.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 18, 2008 12:56 pm Soo desu...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 18, 2008 1:02 pm Solid627 wrote: Does it ever occur to us why the girl Ayanami Rei looks like Ayanami Rei?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 18, 2008 1:04 pm *whistles* Teeeeeeeeechnically she's only as much of an Angel as the rest of the human race.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 18, 2008 1:14 pm *whistles* Teeeeeeeeechnicaly, I already made the point that the distinction is irrelevant, because angels have human souls anyway, just not human biologies.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Aug 18, 2008 1:28 pm Is it just me, or have people around here completely forgotten the mark of the troll?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 18, 2008 1:33 pm Formless_One: A little less sassing other members if you don't mind. This isn't the first time you've done so. BobBQ: I figured as much, but I want to at least give Solid627 the benefit of the doubt.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Aug 18, 2008 1:35 pm Is it just me, or have people around here completely forgotten the mark of the troll?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 18, 2008 5:03 pm BobBQ wrote: Is it just me, or have people around here completely forgotten the mark of the troll?
